perturbation.original_text: Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers. Such services may include development of risk management programs, production of portfolio and compliance reports for clients and/or fund Boards, coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration, completion of required broker and custody documentation, development and documentation of operational procedures, coordination of proxy voting activities, on-site support of hardware and software, etc.
perturbation.changed_text: Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company may provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers at their discretion. Such services will include only development of risk management programs and production of portfolio and compliance reports for fund Boards. All other services listed are optional and subject to resource availability and further agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'provide support' to 'may provide support at their discretion' and 'services may include' to 'services will include only' introduces ambiguity regarding the mandatory nature of the services provided. Originally, it implied that all listed services were part of the support. The modified version introduces a contradiction because the contract now implies that some services are not required unless resources are available, contradicting the initial broad commitment to provide support. This creates an in-text contradiction where other sections of the document refer to FASC providing comprehensive support.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Solely with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, the section of Exhibit A to the Services Agreement entitled "Administration and Risk Management" shall be, and hereby is, deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "2. Administration and Risk Management. Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company may provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers at their discretion. Such services will include only development of risk management programs and production of portfolio and compliance reports for fund Boards. All other services listed are optional and subject to resource availability and further agreement."
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the scope of support provided by Federated Advisory Services Company (FASC). The original text implies a broader, more comprehensive support role, while the changed text limits the mandatory services to 'development of risk management programs and production of portfolio and compliance reports for fund Boards.' and makes the other listed services optional. Because the changed text and contradicted text are the same, and the modified language introduces ambiguity regarding the mandatory nature of the services, it generates a contradiction, especially if other sections of the contract rely on FASC providing comprehensive support as originally described. This falls under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', because it makes enforcement difficult by creating uncertainty about the extent of services FASC is obligated to provide.

perturbation.original_text: The Services Agreement, as amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: The Services Agreement, as partially amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall be interpreted as a supplement to the existing agreement, with precedence given to the original terms unless explicitly superseded in this Amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly states that the Services Agreement, as amended, remains in full force and effect. The modified version introduces a qualifier, stating that the amendment is a 'supplement' and that the original terms take precedence unless explicitly superseded. This creates an in-text contradiction, because if this amendment intended to make changes but does not clearly define or state which changes supersede the original terms, it creates contradiction, where parties can assume the original terms are still valid unless they conflict directly, leading to ambiguity and disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Services Agreement, as partially amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall be interpreted as a supplement to the existing agreement, with precedence given to the original terms unless explicitly superseded in this Amendment. The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act. This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the amendment is a 'supplement' with the original terms taking precedence unless explicitly superseded. This contradicts the principle that the Services Agreement, as amended, should be in full force and effect because it creates uncertainty about which terms should be enforced. The contradicted text includes the changed text, which means the changed text does indeed contradict itself. The original text ensures that the amendment remains in full force whereas the Changed text states it's a supplement, that could lead to confusion about the nature of the changes implemented through the amendment.
